{{Infobox character
|nom=Caterpillar
|apparition=Appears in book 1
|status=Unknown
}}

== Biography ==

The Caterpillar is a creature who speaks in a languid, sleepy voice while smoking a hookah. Upon meeting Alice, the Caterpillar questioned her identity, asking "Who are you?" Alice subsequently recited the poem "You are Old, Father William" to the Caterpillar, though her recitation differed from the original composition.

== Relationships ==

'''[[Alice]]''' — acquaintance. The Caterpillar and Alice appear to have encountered one another during Alice's explorations. Alice recited the poem "You are old, Father William" to the creature, with the words coming out differently than she anticipated.

''Evolution:''
* [[Alice]] — Acquaintance (ch.5)
== References ==

- Alice's Adventures in Wonderland

[[Category:Characters]]
[[Category:Secondary Characters]]
[[Category:Personnages du Tome 1]]
